parsed_review.summary: CP00-798 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk C, 150 units, RP26.P03.M06.S11-RP26.P04.M03.S18) is a descriptor-only API-evidence-tail + UI-foundation pack, and its descriptor-verified production_ready claim is well supported by everything inspectable at review time. Source was inspected via Read/Grep/Glob (not summary-only). Metadata is consistent across the prompt, review-baseline, review-request, manifest, registry binding, committed contract, and the live closeout-pack-plan: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP798_PACK_BINDING (CP00-798, 150 units, first RP26.P03.M06.S11 / last RP26.P04.M03.S18, upstream CP00-797, next CP00-799 / RP26.P04.M03.S19, flag enterprise_saas_api_evidence_ui_foundation_descriptor_verified) matches manifest.plan_binding_snapshot and contract current_pack/latest_pack/latest_projection exactly. The live plan live_state (last closed CP00-797, next_subphase RP26.P03.M06.S11) confirms CP00-798 is the correct next pack, and source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65... matches the manifest; CP00-799 begins at RP26.P04.M03.S19 with no gap/overlap. Deliverable distribution {test:15,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:10,implementation:52,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3}=150 was confirmed by direct count of the baseline units and equals ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP798_REQUIREMENTS.deliverable_counts and the prompt; micro-title distribution sums to 150; row arrays (API_CONTRACT_ROWS=10, API_TYPE_ROWS=14, API_IMPLEMENTATION_ROWS=22, UI_FOUNDATION_ROWS=20) map exactly to the per-section slices (M06=slice(10,20)=10 correctly taking S11-S20 after CP797's S01-S10; M07=22; M08/M09=slice(0,20)=20; M10=API_CONTRACT_ROWS=10; M00=10; M01/M02=20; M03=18). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code: freezeResult spreads ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION into every descriptor/packet/case-set, createCaseSet stamps each row descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false with no tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads, and validateDescriptor rejects runtime execution, payload inclusion, SSO/MFA/SCIM runtime, cross-tenant routes, key/secret material, runtime permission eval/writes, audit writes, permission-decision-detail/audit-event-body exposure, UI-runtime/API-handler execution, real tenant data, and enterprise-trust claims; all UI_FOUNDATION rows carry executes_ui_runtime=false. The three CP798 tests assert coverage.errors empty + by_deliverable equality + by_phase (P03=82, P04=68) against the LIVE plan, descriptor validation empty + authority booleans (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true) + M07 API rows that never execute handlers/include raw payloads/evaluate-or-write permission/write audit + M01 UI rows that never execute UI runtime/write product state/expose permission-decision or audit-event bodies + Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false + Claude read_only/promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false + handoff to_pack_id, plus contract promotion and CP796/CP797 historical projection retention. index.js export* surfaces all CP798 symbols (binding, requirements, descriptor, SSO/SCIM descriptors), the validator script scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs is retargeted to CP798, the README has the cp00-798 entry, and the committed contract validation marker is valid=true/errors=[] with projections cp788-cp798. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer (review-request read_only=true, tools Read,Grep,Glob), not final approver, and Hermes is used only as a descriptor-only control-plane evidence/review-receipt pattern (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write), not embedded product runtime - matching the stated authority_boundary. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid non-empty JSON), model claude-opus-4-8 / effort max / read_only, tools Read,Grep,Glob with forbidden_tools_present=false, prompt 2691 bytes within the 25600 limit, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used, summary_only_review_allowed=false, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[], and no invalid review attempts. The pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, descriptor_only=true, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending. Two non-blocking P3 observations are recorded (closeout finalization artifacts not yet present at review time, so the 10 validation commands could not be independently re-run by this read-only review - deferred to and deterministically gated by npm run closeout-pack:validate; and a cosmetic changed-file-scope.json double-classification carryover). No P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.title: Closeout finalization artifacts absent at review time; the 10 validation commands are not independently re-runnable by this read-only review
findings.details: At review time docs/closeout-packs/cp00-798/ contains only manifest.json; the required_pack_artifacts command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are absent (Glob confirms manifest.json is the only file present, and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-798/ has no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json yet). This is expected workflow ordering: the manifest is still status=in_progress with production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending / review_receipt_ref=null, so the closeout-finalization artifacts are produced after this review receipt. Because this reviewer is read-only with no Bash, the 10 expected validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, and git diff --check) could not be independently executed; their results are not yet captured in-repo. The prior pack CP00-797 closed with the identical observation (CP797-001), and the in-repo command-evidence.json/adjudication.md/construction-inspection.json for CP00-797 confirm this ordering. Pack metadata, implementation, tests, validators, contract promotion, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are all internally consistent and independently verified here, so this is workflow ordering rather than a defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required before this receipt. During finalization, generate command-evidence.json recording all 10 required commands at exit_code 0, plus adjudication.md (Production ready after adjudication: yes) and construction-inspection.json (verdict PASS), then run npm run closeout-pack:validate to deterministically gate the production_ready flip. The independent command verification this read-only review could not perform is covered by that gate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected workflow ordering; finalization artifacts are produced after this receipt and are deterministically gated by closeout-pack:validate. Non-blocking; deferred to closeout finalization.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json cosmetically double-classifies docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is listed both under untracked_implementation_files (line 160) and under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 166). This is the same labeling carryover flagged for CP00-797 (CP797-002). The authoritative scope fields remain correct: active_pack_implementation_refs lists exactly the seven real pack files (contract, README, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, model.test.js, validator script), active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[], and the manifest worktree_transition_policy treats new-session-handoff.md as unrelated while only requiring docs/closeout-packs/cp00-798 and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-798 as active untracked files (the pack's own evidence dir docs/closeout-packs/cp00-798/ is correctly listed only once, under untracked_implementation_files). Pack scope integrity and closeout are unaffected, and no unrelated dirty file (e.g., progress-control-room files, workbook/* matter docs) is pulled into the pack's authoritative refs.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, classify each untracked file as exactly one of active-implementation or unrelated so docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is not listed under both. Cosmetic; no closeout action required for CP00-798.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic observation in a generated review-harness artifact; authoritative scope fields are correct. Deferred to a later generator cleanup.
